body.title: Add purpose, purpose_category, purpose_embedding fields to engram metadata
body.result: PURPOSE_CATEGORIES + purpose/purpose_category/purpose_embedding properties + set_purpose() on Memory dataclass; metadata-based, no DB migration
body.description:
Engrams currently have no self-describing 'why does this memory exist?' field, making inspection require reading full content. Add three fields to engram metadata: purpose (free-text sentence), purpose_category (controlled vocab enum), purpose_embedding (pgvector vector for semantic search). Define starter vocabulary for purpose_category: skill, fact, preference, constraint, decision, experience, procedure.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/models.py, clan schema migration, possibly wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (Postgres-only, no SQLite); theigors/rules/memory (memory shape changes need test coverage)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: three new nullable fields on clan.memories (or engram table), starter vocabulary definition, schema migration. Out of scope: populating these fields (that's the annotator habit), changing existing engram query paths.
**Test plan:** Migration runs cleanly on empty values; can store and retrieve all three fields; purpose_embedding accepts pgvector values; existing engram tests still pass.
